headline: DC/DOX Loads Documentary Lineup with Boeing Expose and Tech Frontier Exploration
lede: The nation's premier documentary festival prepares to unleash nine world premieres, with Rory Kennedy's Boeing investigation taking center stage in Washington, D.C. this June.
body:
The fourth iteration of DC/DOX promises to deliver a provocative slate of nonfiction storytelling, with filmmaker Rory Kennedy's high-profile Boeing documentary *Freefall: A Reckoning for Boeing* leading the charge.

Kennedy, known for her incisive investigative work, will spotlight the aerospace giant's recent safety and operational challenges through a lens of systemic corporate accountability. Her film represents a critical examination of the industry's most controversial corporate narrative in recent years.

Marilyn Ness's *The Endless Frontier* offers another compelling world premiere, likely exploring technological innovation and its societal implications. The festival's lineup signals a commitment to substantive, deeply researched documentary storytelling that illuminates complex contemporary issues.

The June 11-14 festival will showcase nine world premieres, demonstrating DC/DOX's growing reputation as a premier platform for documentary filmmakers pushing boundaries of journalistic and cinematic storytelling.
